title: podman secrets should not be world readable / add options to set file uid / gid
body:
**TLDR: Already a feature** - read the [`run` manpage](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#secret-secret-opt-opt) Luke !!! ;o)

---

### Feature request description

I'm currently experimenting with [`step-ca`](https://smallstep.com/docs/tutorials/docker-tls-certificate-authority) & `podman` secrets & noticed the secret is world readable:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/48e4921a-da0f-4c24-a99a-d8b1bb6293c4)


### Suggest potential solution

If there were options to set the `gid` / `uid` on the secret (& one of these options were set) the secret could have file permissions of `440` by default & be a bit more secure at rest (i.e not being world readable)

### Additional context

For anyone coming across this also experimenting with`step-ca` - this is how I run it with a strong secret (`378,000` chars) over `netbird` (a nice `wireguard` solution) - with proper `mount` privileges for the `secret`:
```
iface=wt0
ctr=stepca
ip=$(ip -f inet addr show $iface | sed -En -e 's/.*inet ([0-9.]+).*/\1/p')
repo=docker.io/smallstep/step-ca:hsm
dns="ca.domain.com,$ip,localhost,127.0.0.1"
email="user@domain.com"
ca="My CA"

bytes=378000
openssl rand -base64 $bytes | podman secret create --replace $ctr -

podman run -d --replace \
    --name $ctr \
    --hostname $ctr \
    --secret source=$ctr,type=mount,uid=1000,gid=1000,mode=440 \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_NAME=$ca" \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_DNS_NAMES=$dns" \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_PROVISIONER_NAME=$email" \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_SSH=true" \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_ACME=true" \
    --env "DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/$ctr" \
    --cap-drop ALL \
    --restart always \
    --privileged \
    --label "io.containers.autoupdate=registry" \
    -v ${HOME}/volumes/$ctr/config:/home/step:Z \
$repo
```

* I'm running a `rootful` container with the `--privileged` flag so the `tpm` device on my workstation is available (& is probably the solution I'll use)

comments.body:
The options `mode`, `uid` and `gid` are already available

```
$ echo secretdata | podman secret create mysecret -
6d0c4000f7ac70144301d7bfc
$ podman run --rm --secret source=mysecret,type=mount,uid=1001,gid=1001,mode=440 docker.io/library/alpine ls -l /run/secrets/mysecret
-r--r-----    1 1001     1001            11 May  4 18:14 /run/secrets/mysecret
$ podman --version
podman version 5.4.2
```



Reference:
`podman run` man page: [`--secret`](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#secret-secret-opt-opt)

sorry my mistake - I didn't look at `run` options - only the `secret` [manpage](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-secret-create.1.html)

* `podman` is awesome ;o)
I'll make a PR with an example mount options on the [`secret` manpage](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-secret-create.1.html) & link `run` options under "see also"
